findings.title: Import boundaries clean — one late import, no back-imports
findings.description: Imports: json, dataclasses, datetime (stdlib). One late import inside identify_entities(): marketmind.config.asset_class_routing (route_asset_class) — this is a config module (data module, leaf in DAG). No imports from gateway/, pipeline/ (siblings), shadows/, or glue layer. The late import pattern avoids circular imports at module load time.

findings.title: Data flow: consumes lesson dicts, produces entity memories
findings.description: entity_memory (L4) sits between reflection_agent (L3) and expertise_discovery (L6) in the Phase I DAG. Data flow: update_entity_memory() receives lesson dict (from StructuredLesson via vars()) → stores in entity_memories table → returns EntityMemory. load_entity_memories() loads from store → returns dict of EntityMemory. expertise_discovery reads EntityMemory for methodology injection. No circular data flow.

findings.title: async functions contain no await — could be synchronous
findings.description: load_entity_memories() (line 80) and update_entity_memory() (line 111) are declared async but contain no await expressions. The store methods (get_entity_memory, update_entity_memory) are synchronous. This means callers must await these functions unnecessarily. Not a bug, but adds unnecessary complexity to call sites.
findings.remediation: LOW priority. Either make these functions synchronous (remove async) or add a comment explaining that async is reserved for future async store operations. Current behavior is correct but misleading.

findings.title: Entity identification is deterministic and testable
findings.description: identify_entities() is a pure function: hypothesis_text + optional tickers → list of (entity_id, entity_type) tuples. No side effects, no I/O, no randomness. This makes it trivially testable and safe to call from any context. The late import of route_asset_class is only triggered when tickers are provided.

findings.title: decay_memories is a stub — returns 0
findings.description: Line 187-194: decay_memories() returns 0 with a comment noting it needs store.list_all_entities(). This is a known placeholder. It does not block any existing functionality. The comment documents the dependency clearly.
findings.remediation: Implement when list_all_entities() is available on LearningStore. Not blocking for Phase I integration.

findings.title: Rolling accuracy calculation is numerically stable
findings.description: Line 167-170: mem.avg_prediction_accuracy = round((old_acc * (n - 1) + success) / n, 4). Uses cumulative moving average formula, avoiding storing all historical outcomes. The analysis_count is used as n (including the new analysis), so the formula is correct: new_avg = (old_avg * (n-1) + new_value) / n.

notes: Clean integration. The async-without-await pattern is cosmetic. The decay_memories stub is a documented placeholder. Core data flow (lesson → entity memory → methodology) is correctly architected.
